title: bo_decision record
description: The UCB-like ACQUISITION decision over reviewed candidates for the DeepScientist-inspired idea-level BO loop: which candidate was selected to try next and why (and why the others were not). record_id = decision_id; append-only (insert). ADVISORY — a bo_decision never blocks a transition or finalize, and never alters idea_select.valid. QUEST-LOCAL ONLY. This is an LLM-reviewer surrogate + UCB-like acquisition, NOT full statistical Bayesian optimization.

quest_id.description: owning quest; REQUIRED — decisions are quest-local

candidate_refs.description: the candidate refs considered in this decision (quest-local)

review_refs.description: the bo_review review_ids whose valuations fed the acquisition

selected_candidate_ref.description: the chosen next candidate (null/absent if no candidate scored)

acquisition_method.description: e.g. ucb_like_v1 — the documented acquisition rule used

acquisition_config.description: the acquisition knobs used, e.g. {w_u:1, w_q:1, kappa:1} for ucb_official_v1 or {beta:0.5} for ucb_like_v1

acquisition_scores.description: per-candidate score breakdown: [{candidate_ref, score, eligible, rejection_reason, ...}] including hard-gate eligibility for transparency

selection_rationale.description: why the selected candidate beat the others (or the explicit skip reason for a single viable candidate)

decision_kind.description: which research move this acquisition decided. 'idea-selection' is DECISIVE (binds idea_select.retained_candidate). The later next-move kinds (experiment-/opportunity-/stop-write-finalize-/next-move-selection) bind orchestrator ROUTING to the winner's route_target. Default 'idea-selection'.
